reviewed.reasoning: The fallback function performs an unrestricted delegatecall to fibonacciLibrary, letting anyone execute library functions in the context of FibonacciBalance. Because of the different storage layout, a caller can invoke setStart() to overwrite the fibonacciLibrary address (slot 0) and gain full control; therefore a critical state-changing action lacks access control.

reviewed.reasoning: The function sendTo uses tx.origin instead of msg.sender for authorization, allowing an attacker to call the function through an intermediate contract while keeping tx.origin equal to the owner, thus bypassing proper access control.

contracts.llm_response: {"reviewed": [{"original_finding": {"vuln_class": "access_control"}, "decision": "confirmed", "reasoning": "The function sendTo uses tx.origin instead of msg.sender for authorization, allowing an attacker to call the function through an intermediate contract while keeping tx.origin equal to the owner, thus bypassing proper access control."}]}

reviewed.reasoning: Functions execute() and confirm() forward Ether and gas to arbitrary addresses with .call() before internal state (e.g., deletion of m_txs) is finalised, so an external contract owned by an authorised signer can re-enter the wallet and interfere with its logic.
The initialiser initWallet() is only guarded by only_uninitialized, meaning anyone can call it on an uninitialised proxy and set themselves as owner(s), giving unauthorised control over the wallet.
The contract uses now (block.timestamp) in today() to enforce and reset the daily spending limit, so miners can influence whether the limit resets by manipulating the timestamp.

contracts.llm_response: {"reviewed": [{"original_finding": {"vuln_class": "reentrancy"}, "decision": "confirmed", "reasoning": "Functions execute() and confirm() forward Ether and gas to arbitrary addresses with .call() before internal state (e.g., deletion of m_txs) is finalised, so an external contract owned by an authorised signer can re-enter the wallet and interfere with its logic."}, {"original_finding": {"vuln_class": "access_control"}, "decision": "confirmed", "reasoning": "The initialiser initWallet() is only guarded by only_uninitialized, meaning anyone can call it on an uninitialised proxy and set themselves as owner(s), giving unauthorised control over the wallet."}, {"original_finding": {"vuln_class": "timestamp_dependency"}, "decision": "confirmed", "reasoning": "The contract uses now (block.timestamp) in today() to enforce and reset the daily spending limit, so miners can influence whether the limit resets by manipulating the timestamp."}]}
